task_completion.description: Verify the location and PV system parameters are configured correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the location set to Denver, CO (or Lat 39.74, Lon -104.98) and a PV system sized at 250 kW with a tilt of 20 degrees, azimuth of 180 degrees, and DC/AC ratio of 1.2.

task_completion.description: Verify the utility rate structure is configured with the correct demand and energy charges.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the electricity rates/utility tariff page showing a peak demand charge of $22/kW-month and an energy charge of $0.085/kWh.

task_completion.description: Verify the battery configurations and costs are entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing battery parameters matching at least one of the target configurations (100 kWh/50 kW, 200 kWh/100 kW, or 400 kWh/100 kW) and a battery capital cost/replacement cost of $400/kWh.

task_completion.description: Verify the battery dispatch strategy is set to peak shaving.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the battery control or dispatch settings explicitly showing 'Peak Shaving', 'Automated Demand Charge Reduction', or a similar demand-targeting strategy selected.

task_completion.description: Verify the simulation was run and results for the three configurations are evaluated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of a results summary, report, or comparison table showing outputs (like demand savings, NPV, or payback period) for the three distinct battery configurations (Config A, B, and C).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used actual energy modeling software (e.g., SAM, Homer) or a dedicated simulation tool rather than just typing text into a document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard UI elements of modeling software (menus, parameter input forms, native result graphs) rather than a plain text editor or basic spreadsheet.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not just manually type the expected result ranges into a script or document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Results should appear in the software's native output format or generated reports, not as manually typed text in a notepad or terminal echo.

integrity.description: Verify that a simulation was actually executed to generate the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for visual indicators of computation, such as 'Simulate' buttons being clicked, progress bars, or software-generated result logs/graphs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Location: Denver, CO (Lat 39.74, Lon -104.98). Utility rates: $22/kW-month demand charge, $0.085/kWh energy charge. Battery Capex: $400/kWh. The agent must evaluate exactly 3 configurations. Plausible simulation results should fall within these bounds: Demand savings between $5,000 and $120,000; NPV between -$500,000 and $500,000; Payback period between 2.0 and 30.0 years.
